summary.narrative: Governor Murphy fulfilled several major campaign promises including raising the minimum wage to $15/hour, legalizing adult-use cannabis, and implementing a millionaires tax. His 'stronger and fairer' agenda saw significant progress on education funding and paid family leave, though property tax relief efforts showed mixed results against his ambitious goals.

promises.text: Raise the minimum wage to $15 per hour
promises.evidence: Governor Murphy signed legislation in February 2019 gradually raising New Jersey's minimum wage to $15/hour, with full implementation completed for most workers by January 2024.

promises.text: Legalize adult-use cannabis in New Jersey
promises.evidence: Voters approved a constitutional amendment in November 2020, and Murphy signed implementing legislation in February 2021. Legal cannabis sales began in April 2022.

promises.text: Ensure millionaires pay their fair share in taxes
promises.evidence: Murphy signed a millionaires tax into law in September 2020, raising the marginal tax rate on income over $1 million from 8.97% to 10.75%.
Murphy signed legislation in February 2019 expanding paid family leave from 6 to 12 weeks and increasing wage replacement from 66% to 85% of average weekly wages.

promises.text: Fully fund the school funding formula for K-12 education
promises.evidence: Murphy achieved full funding of the state's school funding formula during his tenure, with continued full funding for K-12 education included in his final proposed budgets.

promises.text: Increase homeownership opportunities and affordable housing
promises.evidence: The administration invested over $50 million in housing initiatives to boost supply and affordability, fulfilling commitments to expand homeownership opportunities.

promises.text: Provide meaningful property tax relief for New Jersey residents
promises.evidence: Murphy expanded property tax relief programs including ANCHOR, but New Jersey continues to have among the highest property taxes nationally. Relief efforts continued through his final budget.

promises.text: Transition New Jersey to 100% clean energy by 2050
promises.evidence: While Murphy advanced offshore wind projects and clean energy initiatives, setbacks including canceled offshore wind contracts and slow infrastructure development put the 2050 goal in serious jeopardy.
